competition consisted of men's and women's [halfpipe](/wiki/Halfpipe "Halfpipe") and [parallel giant slalom](/wiki/Parallel_giant_slalom "Parallel giant slalom") events. The snowboarding competition took place at [Park City](/wiki/Park_City,_Utah "Park City, Utah"), over a five-day period.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-SR-2)
["Snowboarding"](http://saltlake2002legacy.com/sports/snowboarding/). Salt Lake City 2002 Legacy. Retrieved 24 February 2014.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/Olympic_snowboarding_stadium.jpg/250px-Olympic_snowboarding_stadium.jpg)](/wiki/File:Olympic_snowboarding_stadium.jpg)

Snowboard spectator stadium at Park City Mountain Resort.

The **[2002 Winter Olympic Games](/wiki/2002_Winter_Olympics "2002 Winter Olympics") [Snowboarding](/wiki/Snowboarding "Snowboarding")** competition consisted of men's and women's [halfpipe](/wiki/Halfpipe "Halfpipe") and [parallel giant slalom](/wiki/Parallel_giant_slalom "Parallel giant slalom") events. The snowboarding competition took place at [Park City](/wiki/Park_City,_Utah "Park City, Utah"), over a five-day period.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-SR-2)

## Participating NOCs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2002_Winter_Olympics&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Participating NOCs")]
